Lines.FullText: Allows for specifying how a device and its subdevices are to be updated when loading a configuration.

Elements.RawText: Allows for specifying how a device and its subdevices are to be updated when loading a configuration.

Brief.RawText: * @brief Allows for specifying how a device and its subdevices are to be updated when loading a configuration.

Lines.FullText: For each individual device in the setup, the update mode can be specified. The update mode defines how the device is updated when loading:

Elements.RawText: For each individual device in the setup, the update mode can be specified. The update mode defines how the device is updated when loading:

Lines.FullText: - Load: Updates the device. Adds the device if missing.

Elements.RawText: - Load: Updates the device. Adds the device if missing.

Lines.FullText: - UpdateOnly: The device is updated, but not added if missing.

Elements.RawText: - UpdateOnly: The device is updated, but not added if missing.

Lines.FullText: - Skip: The device is not updated. Does neither add nor remove the device.

Elements.RawText: - Skip: The device is not updated. Does neither add nor remove the device.

Lines.FullText: - Remove: Removes the device from the tree, if present.

Elements.RawText: - Remove: Removes the device from the tree, if present.

Lines.FullText: - Remap: Same as load, but removes old device (if present) and uses the new manufacturer, serial number, and connection string to add a new one and update.

Elements.RawText: - Remap: Same as load, but removes old device (if present) and uses the new manufacturer, serial number, and connection string to add a new one and update.

Tags.RawText:
*
 * For each individual device in the setup, the update mode can be specified. The update mode defines how the device is updated when loading:
 *  - Load: Updates the device. Adds the device if missing.
 *  - UpdateOnly: The device is updated, but not added if missing.
 *  - Skip: The device is not updated. Does neither add nor remove the device.
 *  - Remove: Removes the device from the tree, if present.
 *  - Remap: Same as load, but removes old device (if present) and uses the new manufacturer, serial number, and connection string to add a new one and update.

Lines.FullText: opendaq_device_options_remapping Remapping

Elements.RawText: opendaq_device_options_remapping Remapping

Tags.RawText: @subsection opendaq_device_options_remapping Remapping

Lines.FullText: When the update mode is set to Remap, the device is removed and added again with the new manufacturer, serial number, and connection

Elements.RawText: When the update mode is set to Remap, the device is removed and added again with the new manufacturer, serial number, and connection

Lines.FullText: string. This can be used when swapping out a device with a different one of the same make/model. This option will try and load the same

Elements.RawText: string. This can be used when swapping out a device with a different one of the same make/model. This option will try and load the same

Lines.FullText: settings used to configure the original device to the new one.

Elements.RawText: settings used to configure the original device to the new one.

Tags.RawText:
* 
 * When the update mode is set to Remap, the device is removed and added again with the new manufacturer, serial number, and connection
 * string. This can be used when swapping out a device with a different one of the same make/model. This option will try and load the same
 * settings used to configure the original device to the new one.

Lines.FullText: When loading, the new manufacturer + serial number combination will have priority over the new connection string. In case a device with

Elements.RawText: When loading, the new manufacturer + serial number combination will have priority over the new connection string. In case a device with

Lines.FullText: the new manufacturer + serial number combination is not found, the new connection string will be used as fallback.

Elements.RawText: the new manufacturer + serial number combination is not found, the new connection string will be used as fallback.

Tags.RawText:
*
 * When loading, the new manufacturer + serial number combination will have priority over the new connection string. In case a device with
 * the new manufacturer + serial number combination is not found, the new connection string will be used as fallback.

Lines.FullText: If the remapping fails (e.g. due to missing device with the new manufacturer + serial number combination and invalid connection string),

Elements.RawText: If the remapping fails (e.g. due to missing device with the new manufacturer + serial number combination and invalid connection string),

Lines.FullText: the original device will be removed (if present) and no new device will be added.

Elements.RawText: the original device will be removed (if present) and no new device will be added.

Tags.RawText:
* 
 * If the remapping fails (e.g. due to missing device with the new manufacturer + serial number combination and invalid connection string),
 * the original device will be removed (if present) and no new device will be added.

Lines.FullText: Signal->Input port connections will be preserved when remapping, assuming the new device has the same signal and input port structure and IDs.

Elements.RawText: Signal->Input port connections will be preserved when remapping, assuming the new device has the same signal and input port structure and IDs.

Tags.RawText:
*
 * Signal->Input port connections will be preserved when remapping, assuming the new device has the same signal and input port structure and IDs.

Lines.FullText: The device options object can be created using a serialized openDAQ JSON setup. The options are structured

Elements.RawText: The device options object can be created using a serialized openDAQ JSON setup. The options are structured

Lines.FullText: hierarchically, so that options for subdevices are stored in the parent device options. The following metadata

Elements.RawText: hierarchically, so that options for subdevices are stored in the parent device options. The following metadata

Lines.FullText: is read from the setup: local ID, manufacturer, serial number, and connection string.

Elements.RawText: is read from the setup: local ID, manufacturer, serial number, and connection string.

Description.RawText:
*
 * The device options object can be created using a serialized openDAQ JSON setup. The options are structured
 * hierarchically, so that options for subdevices are stored in the parent device options. The following metadata
 * is read from the setup: local ID, manufacturer, serial number, and connection string.
